clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Provide weight management strategies to lower blood pressure in adults with hypertension and comorbid obesity
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Hypertension management in patients with BMI ≥27 kg/m² (overweight/obesity) or BMI ≥35 kg/m² (severe obesity)
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Recommend incretin mimetics or bariatric surgery as adjunctive therapy for BP lowering

navigationPath.purpose: Display relevant recommendations based on BMI category
Present GLP-1 RA recommendation for BMI ≥27
Present bariatric surgery recommendation for BMI ≥35

validationRules: BMI must be ≥27 for incretin mimetics consideration
BMI must be ≥35 for bariatric surgery consideration

rules: If BMI ≥27: present incretin mimetics recommendation (COR 2b, LOE B-R)
If BMI ≥35: present bariatric surgery recommendation (COR 2b, LOE B-R)
Note: Both treatments are adjunctive to existing antihypertensive therapy

categories.name: Incretin Mimetics (GLP-1 RAs)
categories.phrasing: may be effective as an adjunct

categories.name: Bariatric Surgery
categories.context: in combination with behavioral interventions and antihypertensive therapies

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Consider incretin mimetics or bariatric surgery as adjunctive therapy for patients with hypertension and obesity who have not achieved BP targets with standard antihypertensives
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Monitor BP at 1, 3, and 6 months after intervention initiation
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Reassess BMI every 3-6 months; repeat BP at each visit
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if BP remains uncontrolled despite maximum therapy
nextStepRouting.documentation: Document BMI, BP, weight management intervention chosen, and follow-up schedule
